title: ceropteris calomelanos var. chrysophylla
intro: Ceropteris calomelanos var. chrysophylla, also known as the golden-leaved fern, is a variety of the ceropteris fern, a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The medicinal properties of Ceropteris calomelanos var. chrysophylla have been utilized in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin and internal organs. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce swelling and pain, promote wound healing, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, Ceropteris calomelanos var. chrysophylla is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
